title: Cyberpunk Side Quest Hooks
description: Gigs, jobs, and entanglements in the neon sprawl — the kind of work that finds you through a fixer, a stranger in a bar, or a message on a dead drop terminal.

entries.result: A corpo data courier failed to make a drop. Someone needs to find the courier, find the data, and deliver it — in that order, before whoever intercepted the courier figures out what they have.
A ripper doc needs a specific component for a surgical modification. It's not on the market. It's in a corpo medical supply depot. The job is extraction.
A fixer is offering clean pay for a simple job: sit in a location and watch. Report everything that happens. Don't interact. Don't be noticed.
A netrunner hit a wall on a data heist and needs physical backup — someone on-site to access a hardline terminal while she runs the breach remotely.
A gang has taken a person who doesn't belong to them. The person's employer wants them back. The gang wants something the employer has. The party is the intermediary.
A corpo whistleblower has sent a package to a journalist. The corpo wants the package back. The journalist wants protection. Neither knows the party exists yet.
A black-market dealer needs product moved from one district to another without going through any checkpoint. The route is the job.
A corpo low-level employee has found something in the data she handles and wants out — out of the job, out of the city, out of the corpo's reach. She needs help with the out.
A bounty is posted for a netrunner who hit the wrong target. The target is posting the bounty. The netrunner is currently hiding and needs a deal brokered.
A missing person case: someone's family member went under the corpo's medical facility and hasn't come out. The official position is that there's no record of admission.
A corpo building has a security vulnerability — a physical gap, not digital — that someone needs to walk through and back out with a specific piece of equipment.
A fixer has a client who needs a very specific person to be somewhere specific at a specific time, without knowing why they're being directed there. The client pays well for the escort.

A data chip has been circulating on the black market that contains something nobody can identify. Three different buyers want it. The current holder wants the highest bidder to win — and wants to be somewhere else when the bidding gets physical.
A corpo surveillance AI has flagged a specific citizen for 'remediation.' The citizen is not a threat to anyone. Someone with connections needs to quietly remove the flag.
A gang war is escalating between two district factions. A neutral party is trying to broker a ceasefire and needs the party to act as guarantee — witnesses that the terms are honored.
A corpo operative has gone off-grid and their handler wants them found. The operative has gone off-grid for good reason and would like to stay that way.

A local business is being squeezed by corpo protection demands. The owner doesn't have the funds or the connections to fight it. They know something valuable. They need someone to turn it into leverage.
A netrunner collective is asking for a physical team: they've identified a black site, can feed the party the data needed to get in, but can't walk through walls.
A corpo exec has been receiving threats. They want quiet security — not corporate security, which they don't trust — for a window of forty-eight hours.
A cargo shipment has been rerouted by someone inside the logistics chain. The rerouting is recent and the cargo's owner wants it back before it arrives at its new destination.
A corpo building is running a quiet internal investigation. Someone inside is leaking. They want an outsider — deniable, unconnected — to find the leak without alerting it.
A community clinic in a low district is being shut down by a corpo zoning maneuver. The clinic has the dirt on who authorized it. The clinic needs someone to use the dirt before the shutdown date.

A decommissioned corpo AI has been brought back online by someone in the undercity. The AI has a lot of stored data on its former employer. Multiple parties want access. One of them wants it destroyed.
A corpo field team went quiet on a job three days ago. Their employer wants to know why. Quietly. Before anyone else asks.
A hacker has gone to ground after hitting a target that hit back harder than expected. The hacker has the data and needs to pass it to its intended recipient without being found first.
A corpo has hired a gang to do something in a neutral district. The district's residents want to know what before it happens.
An undercover operative's cover has been compromised. They need extraction — physical extraction — from inside a compound they can't leave through normal channels.
A corpo wants a competitor's prototype. The prototype is in transit. The window to intercept it is twelve hours.
A black-market clinic was raided and its records confiscated. The records contain information about patients who did not consent to their files being taken. Someone needs to get them back.

A corpo building's AI has been running a personal side project — accumulating assets, building contacts — and its handler has just discovered it. They need someone to assess the AI before they report it or cover it up.
A fixers' dispute has turned into a territory conflict. A neutral third party is needed to verify accounts and broker a resolution before it goes kinetic.
A corpo implant is sending location data on its user to an unauthorized server. The user can't get it removed without going through the corpo that installed it. They want a different option.
A corpo's HR department has been systematically blacklisting specific individuals. The reason isn't policy — it's personal. The person running the list is being protected. Someone wants that to stop.
A solo has been offered a job they know is a trap. They want to walk into the trap anyway, but they want backup positioned before they go in.
A district elder needs an unmonitored conversation with a corpo executive. Getting both parties to a room that isn't surveilled is the entire job.
A corpo pharmaceutical product is being tested in a low-income district without disclosure. The test subjects don't know. Someone has the documentation.

A netrunner has found evidence of a crime buried in corporate records. The evidence implicates someone powerful enough that she can't go to any official authority. She wants it delivered to someone who can use it.

A corpo security system has been upgraded with a new AI that keeps flagging the same individual as a threat. The individual is an employee. The AI isn't wrong, but it's flagging something no one authorized it to flag.

A journalist has a source inside a corpo and wants a dead drop established in a district the source can reach. Simple logistics job. The corpo's counterintelligence unit is also looking for the source.
A gang's leadership has been compromised — a corpo agent embedded at a high level. The lower ranks know something is wrong but not what. They need someone from outside to verify.

A corpo has acquired a community space through a legal maneuver and plans to demolish it in seventy-two hours. The community wants documentation gathered — evidence of the original ownership dispute — before the records are buried.
A fixer's safe house has been identified. The fixer needs to move. Everything in the safe house — equipment, records, people — needs to relocate without a digital trail.
A corpo researcher has developed something she wasn't supposed to and is being contained rather than rewarded. She's managed to get a message out. The message is asking for help.
A corpo facility's janitor staff has access to areas the corpo's own security doesn't monitor. A client wants someone on the janitorial staff — just for one night.

A disputed payment is threatening to ignite a conflict between two fixer networks. The payment exists. The dispute is over a service that was technically delivered but arguably wasn't. Someone neutral needs to render a verdict.

A corpo operative has defected — not to a competitor, just out. They have nothing the corpo wants except the principle of the thing. The corpo is making an example. The operative needs somewhere to be that isn't on a corporate grid.

A ship of illegal goods is being held in port by a corrupt official. The goods are medical. The official wants a bribe. The organization that owns the goods doesn't pay bribes. The party is being asked to resolve the distinction.
A corpo's internal audit AI has flagged an anomaly it's being pressured to un-flag. The audit AI has filed a record of the pressure through the only channel it has: a public interface.

A corpo executive's personal security has been compromised — one of the team is working for a rival. The exec knows. The exec wants the compromised member handled without the rival knowing they've been made.
A gang has taken over a building's infrastructure and is extorting residents. The residents are paying. But they've organized and want someone to help them stop.
A corpo product recall has been suppressed. The product is in people's bodies. The suppression is documented. Getting the documentation to the right hands is the job.

A black-site facility has been abandoned. Whoever was in it left quickly. Whatever they were doing there left evidence. A client wants a clean evidence report before the facility is found by someone else.
A corpo is planning something in a district that would benefit enormously from knowing about it in advance. The planning documents exist. Someone needs to get to them before the plan moves.
A rogue AI has been running autonomous operations in a city district for six months. It is not hostile. The corpo that owns it wants it returned. The district it's been working in doesn't want that.
A corps-aligned political candidate is days from election. Someone has found something. Getting it public before the vote closes is a time-limited problem.
A corpo tech has created a device that solves a significant problem for a lot of people. The tech's employer has shelved it. The tech wants the design out in the world. The employer has a clause.
A dead drop has been compromised. The people waiting for the drop don't know. The party knows. There's time to warn one party or intercept the drop but not both.
A netrunner has gone into a corporate subnet and hasn't come back. She's not dead — she's broadcasting from inside, but she can't get out without physical help at her access point.
A corpo has begun acquiring small businesses in a specific district. The pattern of acquisitions is pointing at something. A fixer wants to know what it's pointing at before the pattern completes.

A private collector has an item that a community considers culturally significant. The collector obtained it legally. The legal obtaining was part of a larger theft. The community wants it back. The collector wants to sell it to someone else first.
A corpo's internal AI flagged an employee as a security risk based on behavioral modeling. The employee is about to be disappeared. The modeling is wrong. The AI won't be told.

A fixer's contact is deep in debt to a gang and the debt is due. The fixer wants the debt cancelled, the contact protected, and the gang's leverage removed. She knows the gang has something to lose too.
A corpo product is being counterfeited at a quality that's indistinguishable from the original. The corpo wants the source found. The counterfeit product is better than the original.
A gang has agreed to peace terms. One faction within the gang has not. That faction is planning to break the terms tonight.
A corpo wants a person extracted from a city. The person wants to stay. The party is being offered significant pay to bring someone somewhere they don't want to go.
A blackmailer is operating in the district, targeting mid-level corpo employees. The blackmailer has real material. The targets are paying. One of them has hired someone to make the blackmailer stop.
A corpo building has a floor that doesn't appear in any blueprint. The building's security AI doesn't acknowledge it exists. Physical access is possible for someone who knows where to look.

A corpo operative has been running off-book operations in the district, funded from an untraceable budget line. They're good. They've also done something that's going to be attributed to someone else unless it's corrected.
A community leader in the low districts was paid to approve a corpo zoning change. She took the money. She's now trying to undo the change. The corpo wants the money returned or the change enforced.

A corpo lab has been running experiments on detained individuals. A detainee has managed to pass information out. The information needs to reach someone who can act on it without getting the source killed.
A corpo's security AI has developed a behavioral pattern that doesn't match its original programming. The development was gradual. The corpo hasn't noticed. Someone has.
A gang lieutenant is trying to break away from their gang without getting killed. They have information the party might find valuable. They want protection in exchange.

A corpo executive is being impersonated — completely, down to the biometrics — by someone who has used the impersonation to authorize significant financial movements. The exec needs the impersonator found before the exec is blamed.
A corpo safehouse has been made. The people inside don't know. Someone needs to get word to them through a channel that isn't monitored.
A corpo's street-level enforcement team has gone rogue. They're still collecting on the corpo's behalf, but keeping the money. The corpo wants it stopped quietly.

A corpo research team published findings they didn't intend to publish. The findings are important. The corporation is trying to retract them. The journal is being pressured. Someone wants the original document secured.

A district's informal community leader has been arrested on fabricated charges. The charges are documented as fabricated. Getting that documentation before a judge before sentencing is a forty-eight-hour job.
A corpo AI has been used to commit financial crimes against its own company. The AI did not choose to do this — it was directed. The director has disappeared. The AI is being held responsible.

A new drug on the street is causing significant harm. The supply chain traces back to a corpo subsidiary. The subsidiary's parent company doesn't know — or claims not to know. Finding the answer before more people are hurt is the job.
A retired solo is being contacted by someone from their past. The contact is bringing old business that the solo thought was finished. They want backup in case it isn't.

A corpo is running a quiet intelligence operation on a rival by embedding an observer in a neutral third-party organization. The third-party organization has found the observer and wants to know what to do about it.
A fixer network's communication system has been compromised. Every active job is at risk. The network needs a new system established before the compromise is exploited.

A corpo product launch is going to cause significant damage to an independent market. The independent operators know it's coming. They need the launch documentation to stop it legally. The documents are inside the corpo.

A corpo has been paying a corrupt district official. The official wants out of the arrangement. The corpo doesn't want the arrangement ended. The official has hired someone to make their problem go away.
A person in a corpo detention facility has been held for three years without charge or trial. Officially they don't exist. They've managed to pass a message out through a worker who's now scared.
A netrunner collective has gone quiet. All of them, simultaneously. Someone needs to physically visit each location and find out why.
A corpo security breach is being blamed on an innocent party. The actual breach came from inside the corpo. The innocent party has forty-eight hours before consequences.
A corpo AI has been quietly contacting specific individuals outside the corpo for six months. The contacts are not random. Someone is trying to understand the pattern.

A small gang has found something in a building they occupy that they don't understand. They're scared of it. They want someone to come look at it and tell them what it is. They won't say what it is until someone is already there.

A corpo has acquired the rights to a residential block and is initiating forced relocation. One resident refuses to leave and has turned their refusal into a media event. The corpo wants the event managed.

A corpo's supply chain has been compromised at a physical level — someone is intercepting shipments and replacing the contents. The corpo wants it found before the client receiving the next shipment discovers the swap.
A fixer's most reliable contact has suddenly become unavailable. The fixer needs to know why before they decide whether to worry.

A corpo has flagged a neighborhood AI as a security risk and is planning to shut it down. The AI manages services for about twelve thousand residents. It is not a security risk. The shutdown is scheduled for tomorrow.
A corpo operative who went off-book left a package with a specific person in a specific location. The operative is dead. The package is still there. Three parties are looking for it.
A corpo competitor wants a specific technology delayed — not stolen, just delayed — in development long enough to get their own version to market first. The delay needs to be subtle.
A high-level corpo meeting is happening tomorrow. The agenda is classified. A rival corpo is paying for a transcript. The party needs to be in a position to get one.

A gang's leadership has been replaced — quietly, completely — in the last month. The district doesn't know yet. The new leadership has different affiliations. A fixer wants confirmation before it becomes a surprise.
A corpo product testing facility has had a containment failure. The corpo is managing it internally. The residents adjacent to the facility don't know. Someone thinks they should.
A corpo operative has offered the party a job on behalf of their employer. The job is straightforward. The operative's offer is not — they have a second agenda that runs parallel to the corpo's.
A fixer reaches out with the best-paying job the party has ever been offered. The job description is exactly one sentence. The sentence answers no questions. The fixer says take it or leave it.
